uplink.description: MINI sensor message - typical indoor reading
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds MINI sensor message (bytes[0]=1, type string "MINI" at bytes 27-30). expected.data derived by running reference/upstream-codec.js (source of truth) on this input and normalizing onto the vocabulary: temperature u16be/10, humidity u16be/10, co2 u16be; vIndex/tIndex/virusIndex/iaqIndex/moldIndex/dB(noise)/counter -> camelCase extras.

uplink.description: MINI sensor message - indices still calibrating (mold/noise sentinels)
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds MINI message exercising the upstream sentinels: moldIndex byte 0xff -> "Calculating", noise byte 0xff -> "Preheating". expected.data produced by running reference/upstream-codec.js (source of truth) and normalizing.

uplink.description: MINI sensor message - noise byte zero (dB omitted by upstream)
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds MINI message with the noise byte = 0; upstream omits dB when the byte is falsy, so no noise key is emitted. expected.data produced by running reference/upstream-codec.js (source of truth) and normalizing.

uplink.description: Device configuration message (type 0) carries no measurement payload
uplink._note: Upstream decodes bytes[0]=0 as a configuration message (no sensor data). A normalized measurement codec has nothing to emit, so we report an error. Input bytes are the upstream TTN "Device Configuration Message" example.
Defensive guard: an empty byte array yields an error rather than a bare object.
